about_hero_title: We rebuild businesses by rebuilding leaders.
about_hero_subtitle: A premium transformation consultancy for SMEs. We don't just give strategy; we align mindset, culture, and systems to engineer predictable growth.
about_who_body_1: Nucleus was founded on a simple realization...
about_who_body_2: We operate as an elite extension of your leadership team...
about_vision_text: "To be the catalyst that transforms Indian SMEs..."
about_mission_text: To empower founders with absolute clarity...
founder_bio_1: MBA from Delhi in International Business. I had total worked 13 years in corporate.
founder_bio_2: Worked in Delhi, Dubai in International Finance and also worked in one of the biggest private sector Banks in India before pursuing a higher purpose.

nucleus_blog_posts:
[{"id":"post-1","status":"published","featured":true,"title":"Why Most Business Strategies Fail (And What Actually Works)","excerpt":"The problem is never the strategy. It's always the thinking behind it. Here's what we've learned from transforming 50+ businesses.","category":"Leadership","readTime":"5 min read","dateLabel":"Jan 2026","author":"Sanjay Patel","content":"Most founders come to us asking for a better strategy. What they discover is that the strategy was never the problem — the thinking was.

After working with over 50 businesses across manufacturing, tech, retail, and services, one pattern is unmistakable: the businesses that fail to grow aren't missing a plan. They're missing clarity.

Clarity on who they are. Clarity on what they stand for. Clarity on how decisions should be made at every level without the founder being present.

When we diagnose a business, we look at three layers: Mindset (how the founder and team think), Culture (how people behave when no one is watching), and Systems (how work gets done predictably). Fix all three and growth becomes inevitable."},{"id":"post-2","status":"published","featured":false,"title":"The Founder Trap: When Your Biggest Asset Becomes Your Biggest Problem","excerpt":"There comes a point in every founder's journey where their involvement in everything stops being an asset and starts being the bottleneck.","category":"Growth","readTime":"4 min read","dateLabel":"Feb 2026","author":"Sanjay Patel","content":"You built this business from nothing. You know every detail, every client, every process. That's not a problem — until it is.

The Founder Trap hits when the business cannot function without you in the room. When decisions get delayed because they need your approval. When your team stops thinking because they know you'll override them anyway.

The escape route is not delegation — it's developing your team's judgement so deeply that delegation becomes automatic."},{"id":"post-3","status":"published","featured":false,"title":"Culture Is Not a Poster on the Wall","excerpt":"Company culture is not what you write in your values document. It's what happens in your office when you're not there.","category":"Culture","readTime":"3 min read","dateLabel":"Mar 2026","author":"Sanjay Patel","content":"We visit businesses and see beautiful value statements framed on the wall. Integrity. Excellence. Teamwork.

Then we sit in a team meeting and watch the opposite happen.

Culture is behavior under pressure. It's what your team does when a difficult client calls and the founder isn't available. It's whether your people own problems or pass them up the chain.

Building real culture means making the invisible rules visible — and then holding people to them consistently."}]
